extractedText: Uganda's communications regulator warned citizens about Bitchat, a mesh networking app that allows offline Bitcoin payments and encrypted messaging. The app has been used by activists to coordinate protests despite internet shutdowns. UCC Director Irene Ssewankambo stated that use of unregistered communications apps violates the Uganda Communications Act. The warning comes after reports of increased adoption of the app during recent political unrest. Bitchat uses Bluetooth and WiFi to create peer-to-peer networks that function without cellular or internet connectivity. Users can send encrypted messages and make Bitcoin Lightning payments even when the government shuts down the internet.
